name: Draugr
description: The Draugr, also known as a draug, draugur, or draugen, is an undead creature from Norse mythology. They are animated corpses, often guarding their burial mounds or hoards of treasure. Draugr are typically depicted as bloated, discolored, and possessing immense strength, often far exceeding that of a living man. They are known for their malevolence, spreading disease, terrorizing the living, and even possessing the ability to control the weather. A draugr's hunger for life often manifests in attacks on livestock and humans, and they are feared as powerful and relentless adversaries. Defeating a draugr is a difficult task, often requiring specific weapons, rituals, or even the complete destruction of their body to prevent reanimation. They represent the lingering influence of the dead and the dangers of disturbing the natural order.
subtitle: Undead guardians of the Norse world.
appearance: Draugr are typically described as bloated and discolored corpses, often with a dark or bluish hue. Their eyes may glow with an unnatural light, and their features are often distorted and grotesque. They are typically naked or wearing the clothes they were buried in, which are often tattered and decaying. They possess immense physical strength and can be much larger than a living man.
habitat: Draugr primarily inhabit their burial mounds, also known as barrows. They can also be found haunting the areas surrounding their burial sites, such as farms, coastal areas, and even ships at sea. Their presence is often associated with a foul stench and an aura of dread.
behavior: Draugr are malevolent and driven by a hunger for life. They are fiercely protective of their burial mounds and any treasure they guard. They attack the living without mercy, often targeting those who disturb their rest or trespass on their territory. They are known for their loud, terrifying screams and their ability to spread disease and misfortune. They often act alone, but can sometimes command lesser undead creatures.
weaknesses: Specific Weapons (e.g., swords blessed by priests)

keyMyths.title: The Saga of Grettir the Strong
keyMyths.description: Grettir battles the draugr Glámr, a powerful and terrifying undead creature. Grettir ultimately defeats Glámr, but is cursed by him, leading to Grettir's eventual downfall.

keyMyths.title: The Story of Arrow-Odd
keyMyths.description: Arrow-Odd encounters a draugr guarding treasure in a burial mound. He manages to defeat the draugr and claim the treasure, but only after a fierce and dangerous battle.

sources.author: Snorri Sturluson
sources.description: A collection of sagas about Norse kings, providing context for the beliefs surrounding death and the undead.
sources.text: A collection of sagas about Norse kings, providing context for the beliefs surrounding death and the undead.

sources.author: Various Authors
sources.description: Numerous sagas contain accounts of draugr encounters and the methods used to defeat them.
